summary: chunk.py writer leaves stale chunk files when a re-chunk reduces the chunk count (orphaned NNN.toml masquerade as duplicate/overlapping chunks)

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Surfaced during todo:50438e23 (CH apparatus re-chunk). scripts/chunk.py writes chunk files corpus/<trad>/<src>/chunks/NNN.toml as 001..00N for the N chunks it produces, but does NOT remove pre-existing higher-numbered files when a re-run produces FEWER chunks. So when corpus-hermeticum-11 re-chunked 6 -> 5 (apparatus removal shrank the token count below a sub-split boundary), the old 006.toml was left behind: corpus then had fresh 001-005 + a stale 006 whose body overlapped the new 005 (it was the old tail). This looked exactly like a chunker producing overlapping/duplicate chunks and caused a misdiagnosis ('paragraph-group tail bug') before I traced it to the stale file. Current workaround: 'rm -f corpus/<trad>/<src>/chunks/*.toml' before re-chunking any source whose count may drop.

IMPACT: silent corpus corruption (duplicate/overlapping chunk on any re-chunk that reduces count); breaks the round-trip invariant; the stale chunk-id lingers in the DB (node/edges/tags/embedding) as a phantom. The module docstring even claims 'Idempotent: re-runs produce identical output' — false on a count reduction.

FIX DIRECTION: before writing, clear the chunks dir (or after writing 001..00N, delete any chunks/*.toml with index > N). Then re-affirm idempotency. Add a regression: chunk a source to N files, re-chunk a variant that yields N-1, assert no stale file 00N remains. Note: a true fix would also let chunk.py drive its own re-chunk cleanly (no manual rm), which the apparatus rollouts (todo:2957d758, todo:50438e23) currently work around by hand.
